terms.definition: H(X) = −Σ p(x) log p(x); the canonical measure of average uncertainty in a discrete random variable, named after Claude Shannon.
−log p(x) for a single outcome x; the surprise of observing that outcome. Entropy is the expected self-information.
H(p) = −p log p − (1−p) log(1−p) for Bernoulli(p); entropy of a single bit with bias p.
Reduction in entropy after conditioning or splitting: H(Y) − H(Y | X) or parent node H minus weighted child entropies in decision trees.
Entropy measured with log base 2; one bit corresponds to the uncertainty of a fair coin flip.
Entropy measured with natural log (base e); 1 nat = (1/ln 2) ≈ 1.44 bits.
